title: Status Update on the AIM Act and EPA's HFC-Refrigerant Regulations
authors_or_org: Hunton Andrews Kurth LLP (The Nickel Report)
claim_supported: Refreshes atlas L14-033: AIM Act consumption allowance drops from 181,522,990 MTEVe (2026-2028) to 90,761,495 MTEVe (2029-2033), a ~50% cut confirming the 2029 step-down remains scheduled. Also: 'industry initiated litigation ... pending in the D.C. Circuit' challenging the Technology Transitions Rule, with EPA proposing an October 2025 reconsideration adjusting GWP thresholds effective Jan 1, 2026.
locator: Article body, phasedown schedule table and litigation section
notes: New risk not in the original seed: the regulatory trigger E-02 depends on (AIM Act 2029 step-down) is under active D.C. Circuit litigation and a pending EPA reconsideration that could delay or soften it. Should be treated as a timing risk, not just a tailwind.

india_origin_audit.rule: Entirely India-origin sources are ineligible; multinational academic coauthorship is allowed when a non-Indian affiliation is verified.
india_origin_audit.notes: US law firm (Richmond, VA / Washington, DC). Not India.

title: Coolant Distribution Unit (CDU) CDU-1MW
claim_supported: LiquidStack CDU-1MW delivers 'up to 1,350kW of cooling capacity' with vendor-claimed '48% CAPEX Reduction' and '36% Reduction in Energy Used for Cooling' vs air cooling. No unit price published (quote-only). Used as installed-base/capacity-unit anchor for bottom-up arithmetic.
locator: Product page specs and claimed-benefit callouts
notes: Confirms atlas L14-051 (Trane/LiquidStack) is an active, capacity-scaled competitor; no absolute pricing disclosed so unit economics remain assumption-labeled.

india_origin_audit.rule: Entirely India-origin sources are ineligible; multinational academic coauthorship is allowed when a non-Indian affiliation is verified.

title: Data Center Liquid Cooling Market to Approach $7 Billion by 2029 as AI Deployments Accelerate
claim_supported: Manufacturer-revenue liquid-cooling TAM 'expected to roughly double in 2025, reaching close to $3 billion ... before continuing to scale toward approximately $7 billion by 2029.' Two-phase direct liquid cooling 'expected to expand gradually, with adoption accelerating once chip-level TDPs and thermal flux exceed the practical limits of single-phase systems' -- single-phase 'remains the prevailing architecture for most deployments through the end of the decade.'
locator: Article body, market-size and technology-mix paragraphs
notes: Directly corroborates E-02's own painful_job framing (two-phase adoption gated by thermal-flux ceiling, not imminent broad-based volume) -- useful for calibrating how far out real two-phase volume is, independent of the regulatory trigger.

india_origin_audit.rule: Entirely India-origin sources are ineligible; multinational academic coauthorship is allowed when a non-Indian affiliation is verified.

title: Meta shows AI system and liquid-cooled racks at OCP Summit
claim_supported: Meta's Open Rack V3 (developed 'through OCP from the beginning') 'allows multiple liquid cooling options, including air-assisted liquid cooling (AALC) and facility water cooling'; 'liquid cooling will have to become more widespread at Meta' as power demand rises. No two-phase-specific commitment found in this article -- single-phase/AALC is the disclosed near-term path.
locator: Article body, ORV3 and liquid-cooling-rollout paragraphs
notes: Weaker demand evidence than hoped: confirms a real hyperscaler liquid-cooling buyer (Meta, via OCP) but does NOT confirm two-phase adoption specifically, so it should be read as general-liquid-cooling-transition context rather than direct two-phase demand. Flagged honestly rather than overclaimed.

notes: JP competitor/validation-partner evidence: Mitsubishi Heavy Industries is a named immersion-cooling incumbent with an actual named JP hyperscale-adjacent test customer (NTT Data), reinforcing that Japan's secondary-market cooling ecosystem (NTT Facilities DC Cooling Hub per atlas L14-050) has multiple credible domestic players, not just one lab.

india_origin_audit.rule: Entirely India-origin sources are ineligible; multinational academic coauthorship is allowed when a non-Indian affiliation is verified.

title: Asetek Liquid Cooling To Support EU Initiative
authors_or_org: Data Center Knowledge, reporting on Asetek A/S
claim_supported: Asetek's 'RackCDU' is described as a 'hot water, direct-to-chip, data center liquid cooling system' (single-phase), tied to a 2013-era European Commission low-carbon roadmap. No two-phase capability disclosed.
locator: Article body, 2013-03-15
rejection_reason: P4 context citation; claim co-supported by primary sources; retained as working evidence, not a canonical atlas member
notes: Demoted: 13-year-old trade-press coverage of a vendor's single-phase product, used only as generic context to note an absence (no EU two-phase competitor found) rather than as sole support for a specific competitive fact; this is a vendor-marketing-summary pattern, not load-bearing. Downgraded to T3 previously; confirms Asetek (Denmark, EU) is a single-phase D2C player, not a two-phase competitor. Retained as working evidence only.

title: Trade Compliance Flash: BIS Takes New Approach for Export Controls on Quantum Computing, Other Advanced Tech
claim_supported: BIS's Sept 5, 2024 interim final rule imposes worldwide licensing on new quantum-computing/cryogenic-cooling ECCNs (e.g., 3A904 cryogenic cooling >=600uW at <=0.1K for 48h+): 'Licenses will be required even for Australia, Canada, and the U.K.' License Exception IEC is broadly available only for 'Italy and the U.K.'; Japan qualifies 'for certain ECCNs' only -- Taiwan and South Korea are not named as broadly eligible.
locator: Article body, ECCN and License Exception IEC sections
notes: Material new finding: E-04's own JP/TW/KR secondary-market plan (AIST Japan, SEEQC-ITRI Taiwan, KRISS Korea) will likely require case-by-case BIS export licenses for cryo-CMOS/mux/LNA technology transfer, adding lead time/compliance risk the seed's precompany plan did not price in. Also implies deemed-export exposure for any non-US-person collaborators during the 2026-27 academic phase.

Bill 'modernizes and reauthorizes federal quantum research programs.' As of May 1, 2026 publication, full House/Senate floor votes and presidential signature remain outstanding -- not yet law.
locator: Article body, legislative-status paragraphs
notes: Second independent 2030-timing/demand-pipeline source alongside atlas L13-028 (DOE $625M). Framed conservatively: reauthorization is advancing but NOT enacted as of this research date, so treat as a pending (not yet locked-in) funding-continuity signal.

notes: Important competitive-compression finding: SEEQC -- already named in atlas as E-04's closest competitor via the ITRI Taiwan wedge -- is going public at a $1B+ valuation with disclosed IBM and NVIDIA collaboration pipelines, meaning the 'no merchant vendor has integrated harness+cold-electronics' white space E-04 claims is narrower and more contested than the original seed assumed.

title: IBM's Roadmap to Large-Scale Fault-Tolerant Quantum Computing (FTQC) by 2029
claim_supported: IBM Quantum Starling (2029, Poughkeepsie NY) targets ~200 logical / ~20,000 physical qubits running 100M-gate circuits; Blue Jay (beyond 2033) targets ~2,000 logical / ~100,000 physical qubits. Scaling approach: 'multiple smaller chips or modules will be interconnected via quantum links' -- intermediate milestone Cockatoo (2027) demonstrates inter-module entanglement via a 'universal quantum adapter.'
locator: Article body, Starling/Blue Jay roadmap and interconnect-architecture paragraphs
notes: Second independent 2030-timing source (industry roadmap, dated 2029/2033 milestones) confirming the scaling wall is real. Also a genuine substitution-risk finding: IBM's own scaling path is modular chip-to-chip quantum links (Cockatoo, 2027), not more external coax/flex wiring channels per qubit -- this could bypass the exact 'channels-per-loader' bottleneck E-04 targets for at least one major vendor's architecture.

title: The Third Time Will Be The Charm For Broadcom Switch Co-Packaged Optics
claim_supported: 'Meta Platforms' is named as deploying Broadcom Bailly CPO switches, with 'a couple of hyperscalers' (implicitly incl. Tencent) adopting the technology; Davisson-generation CPO chips were 'shipping to early access customers' as of Oct 2025. Thermal problem confirmed directly: earlier laser modules sat 'close to the switch ASIC ... which messes with lasers and can kill them' -- Broadcom's own fix in Davisson is to make laser modules front-panel field-replaceable rather than solve in-package thermal stability.
locator: Article body, Bailly/Davisson deployment and thermal-design paragraphs
notes: Confirms the painful job is real (named hyperscaler deployments exist now) but also shows Broadcom's own architectural response is to move the laser away from the hot zone rather than buy a package-level thermal-stabilization module -- a competing solution path to E-11's product.

CPO described as reaching 'a key inflection point' in the 2026-2031 window.

title: 矽光子商轉元年：台積電、日月光領軍台灣供應鏈突圍 (Year One of Silicon Photonics Commercialization: TSMC and ASE Lead Taiwan's Supply Chain Breakthrough)
claim_supported: TSMC's COUPE platform: '2025年已完成COUPE熱插拔收發模組驗證...2026年COUPE光引擎與CoWoS製程ASIC整合於基板上，傳輸速率達6.4Tbps' (2025 completed COUPE hot-swap module verification; 2026 integrates COUPE optical engine + CoWoS ASIC on substrate at 6.4Tbps). ASE (日月光) ramping FOCoS-CPO for 1.6T specs into volume production in H2 2026 via its VIPack/FOEB (fan-out embedded bridge) thermal-warpage solution. Morgan Stanley forecast cited: CPO switch shipments ~23,000 units (2026) -> ~59,000 (2027, optimistic) -> ~200,000 (2030), 2024-2030 CAGR ~144%.
locator: Article body, TSMC/ASE product-timeline and Morgan Stanley forecast paragraphs
notes: Critical red-team finding: ASE (a named Taiwan OSAT) is already ramping its own in-house package-level thermal-warpage solution (FOEB) into VOLUME PRODUCTION in H2 2026 -- years earlier than E-11's 2028-29 co-development-agreement plan assumed. TSMC's COUPE is also moving to substrate-integrated volume in 2026. This materially compresses the 'no OSAT has solved this' window the idea's competition_outlook described.

india_origin_audit.rule: Entirely India-origin sources are ineligible; multinational academic coauthorship is allowed when a non-Indian affiliation is verified.

title: Grain Belt Express Awards $1.7B to U.S. Contractors Quanta and Kiewit to Build Largest Transmission Line in U.S. History
claim_supported: Grain Belt Express (Invenergy): 800-mile multi-terminal HVDC line across Kansas, Missouri, Illinois, Indiana -- 'the first U.S. Transmission Line to Connect Four Grid Regions' (SPP/MISO/PJM), adding '5,000 megawatts.' 'Nearly $1.7 billion in combined U.S. contractor awards to Quanta Services...and Kiewit Energy Group Inc.' Construction 'targeting commencement... in 2026.'

notes: Genuinely independent second primary US demand source alongside atlas L08-041 (Southern Spirit/Pattern Energy): a different developer (Invenergy), different route, real capital already committed via named contractor awards ($1.7B) -- strong buyer_procurement-grade evidence that multi-terminal HVDC needing DC protection/discrimination is a real, multi-project, capital-backed US wave, not a single project.

Pueblo, Colorado, and the Oklahoma Panhandle'), and Wyoming Intertie ('up-to-1,800 MW... connecting the Eastern and Western Interconnection').

title: Understanding FERC's Order 1920
claim_supported: FERC Order No. 1920 (issued May 13, 2024, amended by Order 1920-A on Nov 21, 2024) mandates that 'each of the transmission planning regions in the United States... undergo long-term transmission planning' plus 'reforms to local transmission oversight and interregional coordination.' Per separate search confirmation, MISO's regional compliance deadline was extended to June 12, 2026.
locator: Article body, order-summary and timeline paragraphs
notes: Independent second official 2030-timing/structural-demand source (a different federal instrument -- FERC, not DOE) mandating the interregional coordination that favors multi-terminal HVDC topologies, which is exactly what creates demand for vendor-neutral DC protection/discrimination and qualification tooling.

NR Electric is exactly that -- a dominant CN LCC/VSC-HVDC protection+control OEM -- which strengthens why E-14's CN chapter is correctly scoped as license-only (test-suite/HIL sales) rather than a beachhead: a merchant relay/discrimination business would face NR Electric head-on in China.

title: Who's afraid of the 300-mile Southern Spirit transmission line?
claim_supported: As of April 17, 2024: Louisiana SB108 (passed Senate 36-1) would deny eminent-domain authority to transmission lines unless 'a majority of their power is consumed within the state'; Entergy Mississippi separately opposed the project before the Mississippi PSC citing potential system 'overloads.' Article frames incumbent-utility opposition as competitively motivated.

Newer 2026 sources (Pattern Energy, DOE) confirm the project is still targeting 2026 construction / 2029 in-service despite this friction, so the risk is real but not (yet) fatal.

title: Additions and Modifications to the Entity List; Removals From the Validated End-User (VEU) Program
authors_or_org: Bureau of Industry and Security, U.S. Department of Commerce (Federal Register / Government Publishing Office)
claim_supported: Effective December 2, 2024, BIS added 'Naura Technology Group Co., Ltd.' and 'Piotech' (plus regional subsidiaries) to the Entity List, with license requirement 'For all items subject to the EAR' and review policy 'Presumption of denial,' destination PRC, citing involvement in semiconductor manufacturing/advanced-node IC development.
locator: Federal Register notice body, Entity List addition table (NAURA, Piotech entries)
notes: CRITICAL finding for F-01: two of the three named CN target OEM customers (NAURA, Piotech) are Entity-Listed with presumption-of-denial licensing for ALL EAR-subject items. AMEC (the third named buyer) is NOT on this list. This materially undercuts the idea's stated CN buyer list and must be treated as a binding constraint, not a footnote.

title: U.S. Commerce Department Bureau of Industry and Security Adopts "50 Percent Rule" for Export Controls
claim_supported: Effective September 29, 2025, BIS's 'Affiliates Rule' (50% Rule) extends Entity List license requirements to 'any foreign entity owned 50% or more by listed companies,' closing the prior 'unlisted subsidiary' gap. Exporters with knowledge of listed ownership have an 'affirmative duty' to verify; critically, 'liability under the EAR can attach regardless of actual or constructive knowledge' (strict liability).
locator: Article body, rule-mechanics and liability-standard paragraphs
notes: Sharpens the F-01-S01 finding: the strict-liability standard means F-01's proposed 'HK-structured IP entity, licensing-with-golden-reference' mechanics (absorbed from rejected sibling F-14) does not obviously provide a compliant shield if any downstream customer (Hengyunchang, Injet, or their buyers) is 50%+ owned by an Entity-Listed party. This is a new, dated (Sept 2025) compliance escalation the original seed's 2027 'export-control counsel gate' did not anticipate.

india_origin_audit.rule: Entirely India-origin sources are ineligible; multinational academic coauthorship is allowed when a non-Indian affiliation is verified.
india_origin_audit.notes: US law firm (Chicago, IL). Not India.

title: Synertia RF Matching Network
claim_supported: Comet's Synertia RF Matching Network (the closest commercial 'digital' matching platform, launched July 2022) uses 'Standard and customized capacitor designs' (i.e., electromechanical/vacuum capacitors, not solid-state switching) with tuning speed '< 500 msec depending on pre-set points,' frequency range 400 kHz-60 MHz, input power up to 15 kW.
locator: Product page, technology and specification sections
notes: Load-bearing technical-differentiation finding, directly fetched from the vendor: the market's leading digitally-controlled RF matching platform is still ~500ms-class electromechanical, two orders of magnitude slower than F-01's <10-microsecond solid-state target. This is strong, dated, primary confirmation that F-01's core technical white-space claim holds against its most likely incumbent competitor.

india_origin_audit.rule: Entirely India-origin sources are ineligible; multinational academic coauthorship is allowed when a non-Indian affiliation is verified.
india_origin_audit.notes: Swiss company (Flamatt, Switzerland). Not India.

title: 两会受权发布丨中华人民共和国国民经济和社会发展第十五个五年规划纲要 (Authorized release: Outline of the 15th Five-Year Plan for National Economic and Social Development of the People's Republic of China)
authors_or_org: Xinhua News Agency (新华社), authorized release of the official State Council/NPC planning document
claim_supported: Official 15th Five-Year Plan Outline (2026-2030) names '集成电路...等重点领域关键核心技术攻关' (integrated circuits among priority fields for core-technology breakthroughs) and commits to raising '产业链自主可控水平' (supply-chain self-reliance). Independent 2026 analyst commentary on this same plan (e.g., dongfang/stockstar-type securities notes, not independently re-verified here beyond atlas L06-051/052/054) cites 2030 targets of 60% domestic-equipment coverage for 12-inch fab lines and 60-70% overall semiconductor-equipment localization -- treat those specific percentages as T2/T3 directional, not primary-confirmed.
locator: Official plan text, industrial-chain and self-reliance sections, published 2026-03-13
notes: Primary official CN government document (T1) confirming semiconductor/IC self-reliance is a named 2026-2030 plan priority -- serves as the 'primary/official' 2030-timing source for F-01's CN chapter (alongside the already-accepted atlas TrendForce/BigGo localization-rate sources). Numeric localization targets in secondary commentary were not independently re-verified against the primary text in this pass and are flagged accordingly.

institutions.name: Xinhua News Agency / State Council of the People's Republic of China

india_origin_audit.rule: Entirely India-origin sources are ineligible; multinational academic coauthorship is allowed when a non-Indian affiliation is verified.
india_origin_audit.notes: Official Chinese state news agency publishing an authorized government planning document. Not India.
